subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Cards
- **Meadow cards** — Large deck of nature observation cards depicting plants, insects, animals, and landscapes.
- **Card types** — Ground cards (brown border), Green cards (green border), Sky cards (blue border).
- **Card requirements** — Most cards require specific icons from previously played cards to be placed.
- **VP values** — Each card has a VP value printed on it.

#### Observation Board
- **Observation cards** — Special cards on a separate board requiring specific icon collections.
- **Observation board** — Displays observation cards available to claim.

#### Path Tokens
- **Path tokens** — Each player has a set of numbered path tokens (1-6) used to claim cards.
- **Token placement** — Tokens are placed on rows or columns of the card grid to claim cards.

#### Other Components
- **Card grid** — A 4x4 display grid where meadow cards are arranged.
- **Campfire board** — Tracks campfire bonus goals.
- **Campfire tokens** — Reward tokens for meeting set collection goals.
- **Icon reference** — Cards display various nature icons (mushroom, feather, paw print, flower, butterfly, etc.).

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Separate card deck** — Sort meadow cards into their respective decks by card back.
2. **Build card grid** — Deal cards face up into a 4-column, 4-row grid display.
3. **Row/column markers** — Mark the rows and columns where path tokens can be placed.
4. **Observation board** — Place the observation board nearby; deal observation cards face up.
5. **Campfire board** — Place campfire board with available bonus goals visible.
6. **Supply** — Place remaining card decks nearby for grid refills.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Each Player Receives
- **Path tokens** — A complete set of numbered path tokens (1-6).
- **Starting hand** — No starting hand; cards are drafted from the grid.
- **Personal meadow** — An empty tableau area for playing cards.

#### Tableau Rules
- **Ground first** — Ground cards (brown) are played directly to your meadow.
- **Green on ground** — Green cards (green) must be played on top of or adjacent to ground cards.
- **Sky on green** — Sky cards (blue) must be played on top of or adjacent to green cards.
- **Stacking** — Cards stack upward: ground → green → sky.

#### Player Count Adjustments
--- 1 Player ---
- **Solo variant** — Modified path token rules and scoring targets.

--- 2-4 Players ---
- **Standard grid** — Full 4x4 card grid and all path tokens.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
1. **Grid filled** — Ensure the 4x4 card grid is complete.
2. **Observation cards displayed** — Observation board has available cards.
3. **First player** — Choose randomly; play proceeds clockwise.
4. **Place a path token** — On your turn, place 1 path token to claim a card.
5. **Play over multiple rounds** — The game spans several rounds until path tokens are exhausted.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### On Your Turn
1. **Place 1 path token** — Choose a path token from your supply and place it on a row or column of the card grid.
2. **Claim a card** — Take 1 face-up card from that row or column.
3. **Play or hold** — Either play the card to your meadow (if you meet its requirements) or add it to your hand.
4. **Play from hand** — You may play cards from your hand to your meadow if requirements are met.
5. **Refill grid** — Slide remaining cards to fill gaps; draw new cards to refill empty spaces.
6. **Optional: Claim observation** — If your meadow's icons meet an observation card's requirements, claim it.

#### Round Structure
- **Rounds** — The game plays over multiple rounds; each round, players take turns placing path tokens.
- **Token exhaustion** — When all players have placed all their path tokens, a new round begins with tokens returned.
- **Game end** — After the final round, proceed to scoring.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Path Token Placement
- **Numbered tokens** — Each token has a number determining its priority or special ability.
- **Row access** — Placing on a row gives access to cards in that row.
- **Column access** — Placing on a column gives access to cards in that column.
- **Blocked spaces** — If another player's token is already on a row/column marker, that spot is unavailable.

#### Card Requirements
- **Icon prerequisites** — Most cards require specific nature icons visible in your meadow.
- **Ground cards** — Usually have no or minimal requirements.
- **Green cards** — Require ground-level icons (e.g., mushroom, flower).
- **Sky cards** — Require green-level icons (e.g., butterfly, feather, bird).
- **Free cards** — Some cards have no requirements and can be played immediately.

#### Playing Cards to Meadow
- **Check icons** — Verify you have the required icons from previously played cards.
- **Covering icons** — Playing a card on top of another may cover icons; plan carefully.
- **VP accumulation** — Each card contributes its printed VP to your final score.

#### Observation Cards
- **Separate board** — Observation cards are on a different board from the main grid.
- **Icon collection** — Each observation card requires a specific set of icons visible in your meadow.
- **Claim action** — Claiming an observation card is an additional option on your turn.
- **Bonus VP** — Observation cards provide significant VP bonuses.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Card Layering System
- **Three tiers** — Ground → Green → Sky creates a natural progression.
- **Icon dependency** — Higher-tier cards need icons from lower-tier cards, creating card chains.
- **Strategic stacking** — Covering lower cards may hide icons needed for future plays.

#### Campfire Bonuses
- **Set collection goals** — The campfire board shows specific icon set goals.
- **Claiming bonuses** — When your meadow displays the required icon set, claim a campfire token.
- **Limited availability** — Campfire tokens are first-come, first-served; races for popular sets.
- **VP value** — Each campfire token is worth bonus VP.

#### Grid Dynamics
- **Sliding refill** — When cards are taken, remaining cards slide to fill gaps, then new cards are drawn.
- **Card flow** — The grid constantly changes, revealing new options each turn.
- **Planning ahead** — Visible cards in the grid help plan future turns.

#### Hand Management
- **Hold cards** — Cards taken but not yet played stay in your hand.
- **Hand limit** — There may be a hand limit (varies by edition); excess cards must be discarded.
- **Timing** — Playing cards from hand at the right moment when icons align is key.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End Trigger
- **Final round complete** — After all rounds are played and all path tokens have been used for the last time.

#### Scoring Categories
- **Meadow cards VP** — Sum the VP values printed on all cards in your meadow.
- **Observation VP** — Add VP from any claimed observation cards.
- **Campfire bonus** — Add VP from any claimed campfire tokens.

#### Winner
- **Most total VP wins** — Sum meadow cards + observation cards + campfire bonuses.
- **Tiebreaker** — Player with more cards in their meadow wins; if still tied, shared victory.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Build a ground foundation** — Play ground cards first to generate icons needed for green and sky cards.
- **Prioritize icon-rich cards** — Cards that provide multiple useful icons enable more future plays.
- **Scout the observation board** — Identify which observation cards are achievable and plan your icon collection.
- **Claim campfire goals early** — Popular campfire bonuses get taken fast; check what is achievable.
- **Balance card value vs icon utility** — A lower-VP card with better icons may be more valuable long-term.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Skipping ground cards** — Without a ground foundation, green and sky cards cannot be played.
- **Hoarding cards in hand** — Holding too many cards without playing them wastes potential VP and icons.
- **Ignoring observation cards** — Observations provide significant VP; treating them as optional is a mistake.
- **Covering key icons** — Playing a card that covers an icon you need for future cards cripples your chain.
- **Chasing high-VP cards only** — Expensive cards with steep requirements may never get played if your foundation is weak.
- **Poor path token timing** — Using high-value path tokens early when the grid has low-value cards wastes advantage.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Which path token to play** — Higher-numbered tokens may grant better access or abilities; timing matters.
- **Grid row vs column** — Choosing which line gives you the best card for your current needs.
- **Play now vs hold** — Whether to play a card immediately or hold it for a better timing when icons align.
- **Observation timing** — When to claim an observation card vs continuing to build your meadow.
- **Campfire races** — Whether to pivot your strategy to claim a campfire bonus before opponents.
